# v1.2 Completeness and Validity Audit

**Date:** 2026-05-27
**Source pack audited:** commander-sdr-kiro-programme-pack-v1_1.zip
**Source baseline:** Commander SDR Final Baseline Document Pack v2.6.2 archived inside the Kiro pack
**Method:** Authored Kiro-pack coverage search excludes the source archive and copied `source_*` documents, so this audit checks conversion depth rather than mere archival presence. It then drives v1.2 remediation.

> Phase 1 status: completed before Phase 2 remediation. The rows below capture v1.1 authored-pack coverage before v1.2 additions. Phase 2 remediation addresses the gaps explicitly called out here.

## 10. Additional gaps surfaced by Phase 1 audit

- Specs #10, #38, #51, #52, #71, #72 and #75 had insufficient or absent first-class Kiro spec depth in v1.1.
- Spec #07 source inconsistency identified: the register names Universal Search, while the source file is Drift and Rule Engine. v1.2 must preserve both by adding Drift/Rule Engine and Universal Search coverage.
- Master inventories were not fully enumerated as enforceable EARS in v1.1: twelve case types, eight signal purposes, ten analytical engines, six workspaces, eleven personas, five authority overlays, three application boundaries, four intelligence streams, four connector classes and OODA dashboard family.
- Surface specifications #65-#70 needed named panel/widget/view enumeration rather than generic surface references.
- Master Technical Specification lifecycle models for tenant, connector and detection model lifecycle needed explicit requirements.
- Vendor API testing scope needed enumeration from the archived API specs directory.
- AgentCore language needed to remain candidate-only and non-binding.
- Feature Registry coverage required an explicit SPEC_COVERAGE_MATRIX document after remediation.

| 504 | binding | Every tenant-scoped table SHALL include `tenant_id`. | partial |
| 506 | binding | Cursor pagination SHALL be preferred over offset pagination. | name only |
| 508 | bullet/rule | Candidate high-volume tables include audit entries, raw ingestion records, rule execution history, email messages, connector run records, and telemetry. | partial |
| 509 | binding | Selected JSONB fields MAY use GIN indexes only where query patterns justify them. | name only |
| 511 | binding | OpenSearch SHALL support broad full-text and cross-entity search; PostgreSQL SHALL remain the system of record. | partial |
| 513 | heading | Domain repository rule | name only |
| 515 | binding | Backend code SHALL use repository/service boundaries aligned to database domains. AI agents SHALL identify the target domain schema before adding or changing a table. | partial |

| 562 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 564 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 566 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 568 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 569 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |

| 571 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 572 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 573 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 574 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 575 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 404 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |

| 886 | binding | ↗ Blocked (dependency not satisfied — not a terminal state) | name only |
| 888 | binding | Open: Sub-case created. Not yet assigned or started. | name only |
| 890 | binding | Blocked: One or more dependencies not in Done state. CAA excludes from NBA ranking. | name only |

| 974 | binding | / `AutoOff` / Analyst / Analyst soft override. CCHE will not auto-assign. Analyst retains or selects assignee manually. TTL-bounded: default 72 hours, then revert to AutoOn. / | partial |
| 975 | binding | / `Locked` / SOM only / SOM authoritative lock. CCHE cannot auto-assign regardless of TTL. Only SOM can clear Locked state. / | partial |

| 1463 | heading | Epic B — Case Action Algorithm | name only |
| 1466 | binding | AC: Given identical parent case inputs (same sub-case fields, same SOMConfig), CAA must produce identical ranking on every invocation. | name only |
| 1472 | bullet/rule | AC: When a dependency sub-case reaches Done, the previously blocked sub-case is included in the next CAA recomputation. | name only |
| 1473 | binding | Test: Create parent case with 3 sub-cases in dependency chain (A → B → C). Assert: only A is actionable initially. Mark A Done. Assert B becomes actionable. Mark B Done. Assert C becomes actionable. | partial |
| 1476 | bullet/rule | AC: Every NBA entry includes adjustedImpact, aisNormalised, typeMultiplier, frictionPenalty, expectedCRSDelta, and rationale. | name only |
| 1477 | binding | AC: Rationale is deterministic text derived from factor values, not generated by Commander AI. | partial |

| 3373 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 3375 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 3377 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 3379 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 3380 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 3381 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 3382 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 3383 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 3384 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 3385 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 3386 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 512 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 514 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 516 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 517 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |

| 519 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 520 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 521 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 522 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 523 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 344 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 346 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 348 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 350 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 351 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 352 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 353 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 354 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 355 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 356 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 357 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 253 | heading | 1. Supersession Rule | absent |
| 267 | binding | Human users may submit evidence, approve governed exceptions, approve high-risk automation, challenge a system decision, request validation refresh, request routing review, prioritise work, annotate records, and confirm… | partial |
| 269 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 271 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 273 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 275 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 276 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 277 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 278 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 279 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 280 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 281 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 282 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 176 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 178 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 180 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 182 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 183 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 184 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 185 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 186 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 187 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 188 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 189 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 1087 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 1089 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 1091 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 1093 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 1094 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 1095 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 1096 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 1097 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 1098 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 1099 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 1100 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 44 | binding | 3. **Metrics must be targetable.** Each priority maps to measurable SDR metrics. | name only |
| 45 | binding | 4. **Boosts are governed.** Priority alignment may influence ordering but must not bypass safety or SLA rules. | partial |

| 250 | binding | Human users may submit evidence, approve governed exceptions, approve high-risk automation, challenge a system decision, request validation refresh, request routing review, prioritise work, annotate records, and confirm… | partial |
| 252 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 254 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 256 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 258 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 259 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 260 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 261 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 262 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 263 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 264 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 265 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 75 | heading | 2. Non-Negotiable Closed-Loop Doctrine | name only |
| 77 | binding | Commander SDR SHALL enforce the following doctrine: | name only |
| 79 | binding | 1. **No manual case creation.** Cases are generated only from normalised risk objects, communication-ingested risk objects, tool-health objects, exposure objects, drift objects, vulnerability objects, control objects, i… | partial |
| 81 | binding | 3. **Prioritisation-only interaction.** Operators may prioritise, annotate, challenge, approve, suppress, or provide evidence. They may not directly mutate lifecycle state. | partial |
| 82 | binding | 4. **Automatic routing.** The routing engine SHALL produce the route, owner, team, approval path, escalation path, and rationale for every case and blocking sub-action. | partial |
| 83 | binding | 5. **Automatic sub-action generation.** The case engine SHALL generate sub-actions from risk decomposition, remediation options, validation dependencies, communication requirements, ownership boundaries, push requiremen… | partial |
| 84 | binding | 6. **Automatic validation.** Technical validation SHALL be system-owned and evidence-driven. | partial |
| 85 | binding | 7. **Automatic closure.** Closure SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur only when all configured closure gates are satisfied. | partial |
| 86 | binding | 8. **Automatic reopening.** Reopening SHALL be system-owned and SHALL occur when any configured reopening trigger fires. | partial |
| 87 | binding | 9. **Automatic communication binding.** Inbound and outbound case communication SHALL bind to a case, sub-action, risk object, external notification, or allocation queue object. | partial |
| 88 | binding | 10. **Audit-first operation.** Every decision SHALL emit a machine-readable rationale and immutable audit event. | partial |

| 26 | binding | Every operational security tool produces **verdicts** — decisions about whether a given activity should be blocked, allowed, quarantined, coached, required to authenticate further, or monitored. Antigena Email blocks a … | partial |
| 30 | binding | They are **claims, not facts**. A verdict is a tool's assertion at a moment in time, made with imperfect tuning, against imperfect signal. False positives are an operational reality. | partial |
| 31 | binding | They are **high volume**. A single Defender for Endpoint deployment in a 10,000-endpoint estate can produce hundreds of thousands of verdicts per day. Storage and processing must be designed for the volume. | name only |
| 37 | heading | 3. Verdicts as Time-Bound Claims | name only |
| 39 | binding | Every verdict carries five mandatory dimensions: | name only |
| 59 | binding | The verdict is treated as a **claim made at a point in time**, not as ground truth. The claim has time-bound validity — a verdict from 30 seconds ago is much stronger evidence than a verdict from 30 days ago. The claim … | partial |
| 61 | heading | 4. The Eight Verdict Disposition Types | partial |
| 67 | binding | `QUARANTINE`** — the tool isolated the activity but did not destroy it. Defender quarantined an attachment; EDR isolated an endpoint; email security held a message for review. | name only |
| 71 | binding | `REQUIRE_MFA`** — the tool required additional authentication to allow the activity. Conditional Access challenged for MFA; Okta required step-up authentication. | partial |
| 73 | binding | `REQUIRE_COMPLIANT`** — the tool required device compliance to allow the activity. Conditional Access required a managed device; Defender for Endpoint conditional access integration. | partial |
| 79 | binding | `AUDIT`** — the tool recorded the activity for audit purposes without taking action. DLP policy in audit mode; identity policies in report-only mode. | partial |
